**Orfevre** ([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): オルフェーヴル, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Japanese "Romanization of Japanese"): *Orufēvuru*; foaled May 14, 2008) is a retired Japanese [Thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") racehorse and active breeding stallion. In 2011 he won the [Japanese Triple Crown](/wiki/Triple_Crown_of_Thoroughbred_Racing#Japan "Triple Crown of Thoroughbred Racing") and was voted [Japanese Horse of the Year](/wiki/Japanese_Horse_of_the_Year "Japanese Horse of the Year"). In 2012 he added victories in the [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen") and the [Prix Foy](/wiki/Prix_Foy "Prix Foy") but was narrowly beaten in both the [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe") and the [Japan Cup](/wiki/Japan_Cup "Japan Cup"). He acquired the reputation of being an extremely talented but temperamental racehorse. In 2013 Orfevre again won the Prix Foy and finished second in the Arc before ending his career with an eight length win in the [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen"). Orfevre was inducted into [Japan Racing Association Hall of Fame](/wiki/Japan_Racing_Association_Hall_of_Fame "Japan Racing Association Hall of Fame") on 14th September 2015.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orfevre&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Orfevre ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language") for 'goldsmith') is a chestnut horse with a white [blaze](/wiki/Blaze_(horse_marking) "Blaze (horse marking)"), bred by [Shadai Farm](/wiki/Shadai_Farm "Shadai Farm") in Hokkaido, Japan. His sire [Stay Gold](/wiki/Stay_Gold_(horse) "Stay Gold (horse)"), a son of the thirteen-time [Leading sire in Japan](/wiki/Leading_sire_in_Japan "Leading sire in Japan") [Sunday Silence](/wiki/Sunday_Silence "Sunday Silence"), was a successful international performer, winning the [Dubai Sheema Classic](/wiki/Dubai_Sheema_Classic "Dubai Sheema Classic") and the [Hong Kong Vase](/wiki/Hong_Kong_Vase "Hong Kong Vase"). Standing as a stud at Big Red Farm in [Hokkaido](/wiki/Hokkaido "Hokkaido"), he has produced numerous important winners including [Dream Journey](/wiki/Dream_Journey "Dream Journey"), [Nakayama Festa](/wiki/Nakayama_Festa "Nakayama Festa") and [Gold Ship](/wiki/Gold_Ship "Gold Ship").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Orfevre's dam, Oriental Art, was a daughter of the [Hall of Fame](/wiki/Japan_Racing_Association_Hall_of_Fame "Japan Racing Association Hall of Fame") inductee [Mejiro McQueen](/wiki/Mejiro_McQueen "Mejiro McQueen") and also the dam of Dream Journey.[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Kenichi Ikezoe](/wiki/Kenichi_Ikezoe "Kenichi Ikezoe") rode Orfevre in Japan while [Christophe Soumillon](/wiki/Christophe_Soumillon "Christophe Soumillon") rode the horse in his European races.

## Racing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orfevre&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Racing career")]

### 2010: two-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orfevre&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2010: two-year-old season")]

Orfevre raced three times as a two-year-old in 2010, winning his first start a 'Newcomer' race (unraced maidens), second in an open class race and 10th in the Grade 2 [Keio Hai Nisai Stakes](/wiki/Keio_Hai_Nisai_Stakes "Keio Hai Nisai Stakes").

### 2011: three-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orfevre&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2011: three-year-old season")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c3/Orfevre_%28The_78th_Tokyo_Yushun%29.JPG/250px-Orfevre_%28The_78th_Tokyo_Yushun%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:Orfevre_(The_78th_Tokyo_Yushun).JPG)

Orfevre after winning the 78th Tokyo Yushun

Orfevre was beaten in his first two races in 2011 but won his next six races. In March he won the [Spring Stakes](/wiki/Spring_Stakes_(Japan) "Spring Stakes (Japan)") at Hanshin Racecourse.[[5]](#cite_note-5) A month later he won the [Satsuki Sho](/wiki/Satsuki_Sho "Satsuki Sho") (Japanese 2000 Guineas), beating the odds-on favourite Sadamu Patek by three lengths.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The race had been delayed for a week and moved from [Nakayama](/wiki/Nakayama_Racecourse "Nakayama Racecourse") to [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo_Racecourse "Tokyo Racecourse") as a result of the [March 11 earthquake](/wiki/2011_T%C5%8Dhoku_earthquake_and_tsunami "2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami").[[7]](#cite_note-7) On 29 May, Orfevre took the second leg of the Japanese Triple Crown when he came from well off the pace[[8]](#cite_note-8) to beat Win Variation in the [Tokyo Yushun](/wiki/Tokyo_Yushun "Tokyo Yushun") (Japanese Derby).[[9]](#cite_note-9)

After a break of almost four months, Orfevre returned to the racecourse in September to win the Grade 2 [Kobe Shimbun Hai](/wiki/Kobe_Shimbun_Hai "Kobe Shimbun Hai"), again beating Win Variation. In October he completed the Triple Crown by winning the [Kikuka Sho](/wiki/Kikuka_Sho "Kikuka Sho"), with Win Variation finishing second yet again.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On his final start of the year, Orfevre contested the [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen") on 25 December, facing older horses for the first time. The field included [Buena Vista](/wiki/Buena_Vista_(horse) "Buena Vista (horse)"), Jaguar Mail, [Victoire Pisa](/wiki/Victoire_Pisa "Victoire Pisa"), Tosen Jordan and Hiruno d'Amour. Orfevre made a late run on the outside to win by three quarters of a length from [Eishin Flash](/wiki/Eishin_Flash "Eishin Flash").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### 2012: four-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orfevre&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2012: four-year-old season")]

Orfevre lost his first two races at the age of four. In the Grade 2 [Hanshin Daishoten](/wiki/Hanshin_Daishoten "Hanshin Daishoten") on 18 March he was virtually pulled up midway through the race before accelerating again to finish second. He then ran poorly when finishing eleventh in the [Tenno Sho](/wiki/Tenno_Sho "Tenno Sho") on 29 April. In June he returned to form to win the [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen") by two lengths from [Rulership](/wiki/Rulership_(horse) "Rulership (horse)").

In the late summer of 2012, Orfevre was sent to Europe to prepare for the [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe"). In September he won his trial race by beating Meandre in the [Prix Foy](/wiki/Prix_Foy "Prix Foy") over the Arc course and distance. In the Arc de Triomphe he started favourite ahead of the Epsom Derby winner [Camelot](/wiki/Camelot_(horse) "Camelot (horse)"), despite being given an unfavourable outside draw. Ridden by [Christophe Soumillon](/wiki/Christophe_Soumillon "Christophe Soumillon"), he was restrained at the back of the eighteen runner field before making rapid progress on the outside to take the lead in the straight. Orfevre opened up a clear advantage 200 [metres](/wiki/Metres "Metres") from the finish, but then veered sharply right towards the inside rail. In the closing stages he was caught by the filly [Solemia](/wiki/Solemia "Solemia") and finished second by a neck, seven lengths clear of the other runners.

After returning to Japan, his next race was the [Japan Cup](/wiki/Japan_Cup "Japan Cup") on 24 November. The race saw a close finish between Orfevre and the 2012 Fillies Triple Crown heroine [Gentildonna](/wiki/Gentildonna "Gentildonna"). Orfevre was triple crown champion (male) the previous year. In the last 200 meters, he appeared to have been bumped off his racing line by Gentildonna who crossed the line a nose in front of the colt. The subsequent stewards' inquiry found that although interference had taken place, the result was allowed to stand, even though they handed down Gentildonna's jockey [Yasunari Iwata](/wiki/Yasunari_Iwata "Yasunari Iwata") a two-day suspension. Orfevre's Arc rival Solemia raced prominently throughout the race, but finished midfield. Despite receiving most fan votes in Arima Kinen, Orfevre's side decided not to field him the race as defending winner, as he had not recovered sufficiently from the Arc and Japan Cup.

At the JRA Awards in January 2013, Orfevre won the [JRA Award for Best Older Male Horse](/wiki/JRA_Award_for_Best_Older_Male_Horse "JRA Award for Best Older Male Horse"), taking 273 of the 289 votes. In the Horse of the Year poll he finished third to Gentildonna and [Gold Ship](/wiki/Gold_Ship "Gold Ship").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### 2013: five-year-old season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orfevre&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2013: five-year-old season")]

On his five-year-old debut, Orfevre ran in the Sankei [Osaka Hai](/wiki/Osaka_Hai "Osaka Hai") at Hanshin on 31 March. He started the 1/5 favourite and won from Shonan Mighty and Eishin Flash. Orfevre was expected to defend the Takarazuka Kinen on 23 June, but a nine days before the race he bled after an exercise gallop. After [exercise-induced pulmonary hemorrhage](/wiki/Exercise-induced_pulmonary_hemorrhage "Exercise-induced pulmonary hemorrhage") was diagnosed, Ikee decided to withdraw the horse from the race and target the Arc again.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

As in the previous year, Orfevre was sent to Europe in the autumn on 2013 and ran in the Prix Foy as his trial race for the Arc. On 15 September he started as 4/6 favorite against eight opponents including [Dunaden](/wiki/Dunaden "Dunaden") after the late withdrawal of [Camelot](/wiki/Camelot_(horse) "Camelot (horse)"). Ridden by Soumillon, he took the lead 200 metres from the finish and won easily by three lengths from [Very Nice Name](/w/index.php?title=Very_Nice_Name&action=edit&redlink=1 "Very Nice Name (page does not exist)"). In the Arc, Orfevre was made 13/10 favourite in a field of seventeen runners. He was restrained behind the leaders by Soumillon before moving forward in the straight and finished second for the second consecutive year, five length behind the winner [Treve](/wiki/Treve_(horse) "Treve (horse)"). Orfevre missed the Japan Cup and ran his final race in the Arima Kinen on 21 December. In front of a crowd of 124,782, he started the 3/5 favourite against fifteen opponents, with [Gold Ship](/wiki/Gold_Ship "Gold Ship") (17/5) being the only horse seriously backed against him. Kenichi Ikezoe restrained the colt in the early stages before moving forward on the final turn. Orfevre took the lead and drew away from the field to win by eight lengths from Win Variation, with Gold Ship in third. Later that evening, Orfevre paraded in front of 60,000 fans in his official retirement ceremony.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

At the JRA Awards in January 2013, Orfevre defended JRA Award for Best Older Male Horse with 176 votes, beating sprinter [Lord Kanaloa](/wiki/Lord_Kanaloa "Lord Kanaloa") in 104. However, in [Japanese Horse of the Year](/wiki/Japanese_Horse_of_the_Year "Japanese Horse of the Year"), he only voted in second place to Lord Kanaloa in a 209-69 margin. In the [2013 World's Best Racehorse Rankings](/wiki/2013_World%27s_Best_Racehorse_Rankings "2013 World's Best Racehorse Rankings") Orfevre tied with [Wise Dan](/wiki/Wise_Dan "Wise Dan") for third place behind Treve and [Black Caviar](/wiki/Black_Caviar "Black Caviar").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Stud record

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Orfevre&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Stud record")]

Orfevre currently stands at the [Shadai Stallion Station](/wiki/Shadai_Stallion_Station "Shadai Stallion Station") at a service fee of [JPY](/wiki/JPY "JPY") 3,500,000.
